author: Tim Rice <tim.j.rice@hackrange.com>
name: csrf_validation
summary: Confirms (or refutes) a wapiti CSRF finding via cross-session token swap. Wapiti's stock check produces frequent false positives because it reuses one session; this probe runs the textbook server-side CSRF tests (no token / garbage token / cross-session token) and reports a high-confidence verdict.

args.--url: Form-POST endpoint to test (typically the form action — required)
args.--login-page-url: URL of the page that renders the form (defaults to --url)
args.--token-field: Name of the CSRF token field (auto-detected from the form when omitted)
args.--credentials: Optional username:password to send with the test POSTs. Use bogus values; the token check happens before credential validation in correctly-built apps, so the response still reveals enforcement.
args.--method: HTTP method for the test POSTs (default POST)
